evidence: Service manual Table 2-7 defines x3 as 900 PPS using 2-2 phase excitation. Table 2-11 gives 1.11 ms constant-speed timing. Runtime record 0 has 0Dh/0Dh accel/decel counts and 1.11 ms-class timer words.
notes: This is one of the two 2-2 Table 2-7 modes.

variant_scheduler_rows: 72B8 index 1 selects the same runtime profile with faster TM1 bytes
evidence: Service manual Table 2-7 defines x2 as 600 PPS using 2-2 phase excitation. Table 2-11 gives 1.66 ms constant-speed timing. Runtime record 1 has 0Dh/0Dh accel/decel counts and 1.66 ms-class timer words.
notes: Index 1 is a firmware variant using the x2 timing record; do not treat it as a separate Table 2-7 mode.

evidence: Service manual Table 2-7 defines x1.5 as 900 PPS using 1-2 phase excitation. Table 2-11 gives 1.11 ms constant-speed timing. Runtime record 2 has 1Ah/1Ah accel/decel counts and 1.11 ms-class timer words.
notes: This is one of the two 1-2 Table 2-7 modes.

variant_scheduler_rows: 72C2 index 3 and 72CC index 5 select the same runtime profile with alternate TM1 bytes
evidence: Service manual Table 2-7 defines x1 as 600 PPS using 1-2 phase excitation. Table 2-11 gives 1.66 ms constant-speed timing. Runtime record 3 has 1Ah/1Ah accel/decel counts and 1.66 ms-class timer words.
notes: Index 7 selects runtime_record_4, a slower 1-2-family profile outside the four Table 2-7 rows.
